title: Replace a Tipper End Stop Assembly
summary: Remove a damaged tipper end stop and install a new end stop using two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite®, inspect the opposite end stop for damage, reinstall any removed panels, and restart the operator station using the referenced procedures.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tipper end stop assembly is damaged and must be replaced to return the station to service.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a substitute for the detailed Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Do not use this runbook as a substitute for the detailed Starting The Operator Station procedure.
Do not use this runbook for post-restart verification beyond restarting the operator station, because the source does not provide those details.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, gain access if needed, remove and replace the end stop assembly hardware, inspect the opposite end stop, reinstall removed panels, and restart the operator station using the referenced procedures.

safety_notes: This procedure includes lockout/tagout and physical component replacement.
End stops typically work in pairs; a damaged bottom end stop may indicate one of the top end stops is also damaged, except in the event of a belt failure.
access_or_tools_needed: Two M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop

system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before replacement work begins.

visual_references.description: Maintenance diagram for the OptiSweep tipper end stop used during end stop assembly replacement.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before performing end stop replacement.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in the lockout/tagout state required for maintenance.
steps.healthy_condition: The referenced LOTO procedure has been applied and maintenance can proceed.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out/tagged out or safe maintenance state cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper cannot be placed in the required lockout/tagout state.
Safe maintenance state cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

steps.title: Gather replacement parts, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before disassembly.
steps.instruction: Gather the documented replacement parts, PPE, and tools: end stop, two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.
steps.expected_result: All required parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement end stop, new screws, PPE, and listed tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Any required replacement part, PPE item, or tool is missing.

actions.label: Verify required materials
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented replacement parts, PPE, and tools: end stop, two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.

stop_or_escalate_if: The replacement end stop is not available.
New M8x40 socket-head cap screws are not available.
Required PPE or tools are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the documented replacement parts, PPE, and tools: end stop, two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.

steps.title: Remove the side panel if needed for access
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and end stop area when the panel blocks service access.
steps.instruction: If necessary for access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and access to the service area is available.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed without damage and the motor/end stop access area is exposed as needed.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, fasteners cannot be removed, or access remains obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the end stop maintenance diagram for orientation to the end stop service area during access and replacement.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed safely.
Required access to the motor/end stop area is still not available.

source_evidence.text: If necessary for access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.

source_evidence.description: Maintenance diagram for the OptiSweep tipper end stop used during end stop assembly replacement.

steps.title: Remove the damaged end stop
steps.purpose: Detach the damaged end stop from its mounting location.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.
steps.expected_result: The damaged end stop is removed from the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Both M8 socket-head screws are removed and the end stop is free for replacement.
steps.failure_condition: The screws cannot be removed or the end stop cannot be detached.

actions.label: Remove end stop screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the end stop and the two M8x40 socket-head screws used to secure it.

stop_or_escalate_if: The two M8 socket-head screws cannot be removed.
The end stop cannot be removed from its mounting location.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.

source_evidence.description: Figure 7-12 End Stop supports end stop location and replacement context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-12 appears on page 155 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_12_1_replacement.

steps.title: Install the new end stop
steps.purpose: Restore the end stop assembly using the specified replacement hardware and threadlocker.
steps.instruction: Mount the new end stop using two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.
steps.expected_result: The new end stop is installed using new hardware and Loctite®.
steps.healthy_condition: The new end stop is mounted in place with two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite® applied.
steps.failure_condition: The new end stop cannot be mounted correctly or required new hardware/Loctite® is not used.

actions.label: Install replacement end stop
actions.exact_action: Mount the new end stop using two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the end stop diagram to confirm the replacement end stop mounting location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The new end stop cannot be mounted securely.
New M8 socket-head screws are not available.

source_evidence.text: Mount the new end stop using two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.

source_evidence.description: Maintenance diagram for the OptiSweep tipper end stop used during end stop assembly replacement.

steps.title: Inspect the opposite end stop and replace if needed
steps.purpose: Check for paired end stop damage that may also require correction.
steps.instruction: Check the opposite end stop for damage and replace it if necessary, noting that end stops typically work in pairs and a bottom collision often means one of the top end stops is also damaged, except in the event of a belt failure.
steps.expected_result: The opposite end stop is inspected and replaced if damage is found.
steps.healthy_condition: The opposite end stop shows no damage, or if damaged it is replaced using the same documented method.
steps.failure_condition: The opposite end stop is damaged and cannot be replaced, or its condition cannot be determined.

actions.label: Inspect opposite end stop
actions.exact_action: Check the opposite end stop for damage.

actions.label: Replace opposite end stop if needed
actions.exact_action: Replace it if necessary using the same documented method.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the end stop figure to identify paired end stop locations and mounting points.

stop_or_escalate_if: The opposite end stop is damaged and replacement cannot be completed.
The condition of the opposite end stop cannot be determined.

source_evidence.text: Check the opposite end stop for damage and replace it if necessary, noting the source statement that end stops typically work in pairs and bottom collisions often indicate a top end stop is also damaged.

source_evidence.description: Figure 7-12 End Stop supports end stop identification and paired end stop context.

source_evidence.text: The nearby text states that end stops, which typically work in pairs. Nearby text states that a collision at the bottom often means there is also a collision at the top, so a damaged bottom end stop may indicate one of the top end stops is also damaged, with belt failure noted as an exception.

steps.title: Reinstall removed side panels
steps.purpose: Restore guarding and station enclosure after replacement work.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.
steps.expected_result: Any removed side panels are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: All removed panels are back in place.
steps.failure_condition: A removed panel cannot be reinstalled or remains off the station.

actions.label: Reinstall panels
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any removed panel cannot be reinstalled.

source_evidence.text: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the station to service after replacement work is complete.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restart procedure has been completed.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted or restart status cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Restart station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station cannot be restarted.
Post-restart condition cannot be confirmed from this source.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

success_criteria: The damaged end stop is replaced.
The new end stop is installed using two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite®.
The opposite end stop is checked for damage and replaced if necessary.
Any removed side panels are reinstalled.
The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
healthy_conditions: Tipper is safely locked out/tagged out before maintenance begins.
Required replacement parts, PPE, and tools are available.
The new end stop is mounted in place with new hardware and Loctite®.
The opposite end stop is either undamaged or replaced as needed.
The operator station restart procedure is completed.
failure_conditions: The tipper cannot be placed in the required lockout/tagout state.
Required replacement parts, PPE, or tools are missing.
The side panel cannot be removed when access is needed.
The end stop mounting screws cannot be removed.
The new end stop cannot be installed correctly.
The opposite end stop is damaged and cannot be replaced.
The operator station cannot be restarted.
Post-restart verification details are not provided by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed or safe maintenance state cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if required replacement parts, new M8x40 socket-head cap screws, PPE, or tools are unavailable.
Escalate if the opposite end stop is also damaged and cannot be replaced during the same maintenance activity.
Escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.
Escalate for any required post-restart verification because this source does not provide those details.
